title: podman stop doesn't recognize "latest" flag
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Trying to stop the latest container (`podman stop --latest`) results in a `Error: unknown flag: --latest`

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run any(?) container, for example the httpd container from the tutorial:`podman run -dt -p 8080:80/tcp docker.io/library/httpd`

2. Try to stop it using the `latest` flag: `podman stop --latest`

**Describe the results you received:**

`Error: unknown flag: --latest`

**Describe the results you expected:**

`[ContainerID] has been stopped`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.17.2
Built:        Fri Nov 12 17:08:25 2021
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:
Version:      3.4.1
API Version:  3.4.1
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Wed Oct 20 16:32:52 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

comments.body: The latest flag does not work on the remote client, this is expected. see https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-stop.1.md#--latest--l
